                                                     UNCLASSIFIED
                                    RESEARCH, DEVELOPMENT, TEST AND EVALUATION, ARMY
                                                APPROPRIATION LANGUAGE

For expenses necessary for basic and applied scientific research, development, test and evaluation, including maintenance, rehabilitation, lease,
and operation of facilities and equipment, $18,708,826,000.00 to remain available for obligation until September 30, 2028.

The FY 2027 Overseas Operations accounted for in the base budget are as follows:

In-theater and in-CONUS expenses that remain after combat operations cease and have been previously funded in Overseas Operations
$3,244,000.00.


                                                              COST STATEMENT

The following Justification Books were prepared at a cost of $337,499.00 Aircraft (ACFT), Missiles (MSLS), Weapons & Tracked Combat
Vehicles (WTCV), Ammunition (AMMO), Other Procurement Army (OPA) 1 – Tactical & Support Vehicles, Other Procurement Army (OPA) 2
– Communications & Electronics, Other Procurement Army (OPA) 3 & 4 - Other Support Equipment & Spares, Research, Development, Test and
Evaluation (RDTE) for: Budget Activity 1, Budget Activity 2, Budget Activity 3, Budget Activity 4, Budget Activity 5A, Budget Activity 5B,
Budget Activity 5C, Budget Activity 5D, Budget Activity 6, Budget Activity 7, and Budget Activity 8.




                                                                                                                                                    Volume 2a - ii

===== PAGE 6 =====
                                                         UNCLASSIFIED
                              FY 2027 RDT&E, ARMY PROGRAM ELEMENT DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARIES
                                              Introduction and Explanation of Contents

1.          General. The purpose of this document is to provide summary information concerning the Research, Development, Test and Evaluation,
Army program. The descriptive summaries are comprised of R-2 (Army RDT&E Budget Item Justification – program element level), R-2A (Army
RDT&E Budget Item Justification – project level), R-3 (Army RDT&E Cost Analysis), R-4 (Schedule Profile Detail) and R-5 (Termination
Liability Funding for MDAPs) Exhibits, which provide narrative information on all RDT&E program elements and projects through FY 2027.

2.          Relationship of the FY 2027 Budget Submitted to Congress to the FY 2026 Budget Submitted to Congress. This paragraph
provides a list of program elements/projects that are major new starts and terminated programs. Explanations for these changes can be found in the
narrative sections of the Program Element R-2A Exhibits.


                                                               New Start Programs:

     Budget Activity     PE / Project          Project Title

     04                0604117A / DO5        Directed Energy Enhancements

MANDATORY FUNDING JUSTIFICATION: The FY 2027 request for Research, Development, Test and Evaluation, Army includes
$18,708,826 thousand of discretionary and $40,000 thousand of mandatory reconciliation for a total of $18,748,826 thousand.

Munitions
The $40,000 thousand in mandatory reconciliation funds in PE 0604182A / Hypersonics are to support the development and test of the
Blackbeard - Ground Launch (GL) low-cost hypersonic strike missile. This effort will assess the potential to achieve a significantly
reduced cost for high-speed strike capability complementing existing Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) capabilities.

The FY 2026 spend plan amount for Research, Development, Test and Evaluation, Army includes $16,565,760 thousand of
discretionary and $1,778,903 thousand of mandatory (reconciliation) for a total of $18,344,663 thousand.

The FY 2025 spend plan amount for Research, Development, Test and Evaluation, Army includes $14,496,467 thousand of
discretionary and $12,294 thousand of mandatory (reconciliation) for a total of $14,508,761 thousand.

Total Program Element                       -     19.886     17.641       8.367           -        8.367      10.494       11.444      10.833         10.938        0.000       89.603
TR5: Missile Defense Battlelab              -     19.886     17.641       8.367           -        8.367      10.494       11.444      10.833         10.938        0.000       89.603

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This Program Element (PE) funds missile defense systems integration efforts for the US Army Space and Missile Defense Command in its role as the Army Service
Component Command (ASCC) to U.S. Strategic Command (USSTRATCOM) and United States Space Command (USSPACECOM).

 USASMDC: Headquarters, Department of the Army General Order 37, dated 16 October 2006, designated USASMDC as the Army proponent for ground-based
 midcourse defense (GMD), the Army integrator for global missile defense, and the ASCC to USSTRATCOM. Upon its establishment, USASMDC became the Army
 Service Component Command to USSPACECOM. Army Regulation (AR) 10-87 Army Commands, Army Service Component Commands, and Direct Reporting Units
 dated 4 September 2007, and AR 5-22 The Army Force Modernization Proponent System dated 19 August 2009 designate USASMDC as the Army specified proponent
 for Global Missile Defense (GMD) capabilities. As the Army proponent for GMD, USASMDC is responsible for developing warfighting concepts, conducting warfighting
 experiments to validate those concepts, identifying capabilities needed to implement the validated concepts, and developing Doctrine, Organizations, Training, Material,
 Leadership & Education, Personnel, Facilities and Policy (DOTMLPF-P) solutions to realize GMD capabilities. As the Army integrator for global missile defense,
 USASMDC is responsible for reviewing programs managed by the Army, other Services, Defense agencies and National agencies to ensure that they are correctly
 synchronized and will ultimately provide the capabilities required by USSTRATCOM and USSPACECOM to execute their global missile defense responsibilities to
 provide protection of the homeland and regional/theater missile defense.

TR5: Missile Defense Battlelab              -     19.886     17.641      8.367           -        8.367     10.494       11.444       10.833       10.938         0.000       89.603
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -          -            -           -          -            -            -             -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project TR5 funds the Strategic Missile Defense (SMD) and Ground-based Midcourse Defense (GMD) force development activities of the United States Army Space and
Missile Defense Command (USASMDC) Space and Missile Defense Center of Excellence (SMDCoE). The SMDCoE is the warfighting function lead and Department
of the Army force modernization proponent to provide concept development and capabilities determination and integration relative to Doctrine, Organizations, Training,
Material, Leadership & Education, Personnel, Facilities and Policy (DOTMLPF-P) for Army SMD and GMD. SMDC serves as the Army's bridge between force
development, technical-development, and operational fielding for ground-based and space-integrated elements of Golden Dome for America. This project will enable
the SMDCoE to compress requirement-to-field timelines, increase collaboration and integration with Army acquisition and experimentation activities, and integrate
requirements and acquisition activities more fully to create a more threat-driven, portfolio-focused approach in accordance with the Department of War transformation to
the Warfighting Acquisition System (WAS). The SMDCoE missile defense force development workforce supports the research and doctrine development from one of the
SMDCoE principal locations in Huntsville, AL; Colorado Springs, CO; and Joint Base Langley-Eustis. As the Army proponent for SMD and GMD, USASMDC serves as
the Army's bridge between technical development and operational fielding. As the Army integrator for SMD and GMD, USASMDC is responsible for reviewing programs
managed by the Army, other Services, Defense agencies and National agencies to ensure that they are correctly synchronized and will ultimately provide the capabilities
required by USSTRATCOM, USSPACECOM and Western Hemisphere Command to execute their SMD and GMD responsibilities.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                      FY 2025            FY 2026       FY 2027
Title: Strategic Missile Defense Experiments, Wargames and Prototypes                                                                        1.873                 -             -

experiments.

Title: Disruptive Concepts and Technologies Development                                                                                         8.144           7.045          7.284

(AMD CFT) for priority programs.

FY 2026 Plans:
This funding enables the SMDCoE to accomplish its responsibility to provide holistic concept to capability development, and
concept and technology assessment for Army missile defense capabilities across the range of DOTMLPF-P. The SMDCoE
will focus its remaining resources to develop and integrate Ground-based Midcourse Defense (GMD) capability concepts as

FY 2027 Plans:

Title: Strategic Missile Defense Models and Simulations Infrastructure                                                                     0.874             -               -
Description: USASMDC is the proponent for multiple models and simulations (M&S) critical to the Army and Joint analysis,
exercise, wargaming, and experimentation communities.

Title: Strategic Missile Defense Operations Resourcing and Support                                                                         1.995        1.096            1.083
Description: Requirement supports the SMDCoE responsibility to provide resources to support underlying operating expenses for
the strategic missile defense force development mission area.

FY 2026 Plans:
These resources provide for limited computational and network resources, modeling and simulation, and operational analysis
required to support Golden Dome for America and related Homeland Defense efforts (to include the National Capital Region
Integrated Air Defense System). These decisions include the acquisition of systems and the development of Concepts of
Operations (CONOPs) that provide the best Joint and Army Missile Defense capabilities to current and future Warfighters.
FY 2027 Plans:
This funding provides essential enabling capabilities that directly support the overarching mission and operational effectiveness
of the Space and Missile Defense Center of Excellence (SMDCoE). The effort leverages economies of scale and optimizes
technology to provide integrated and centralized operational, logistical, manpower, and budget planning support that is
foundational to the SMDCoE's success.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
The decrease in FY 2027 reflects optimization efforts.

Congressional Add: Ground Test for Hypersonics                                                                            7.000   5.000
FY 2025 Accomplishments: Improved the ability to test advanced materials and aerodynamic/control
technologies, including thermal solutions for advanced materials (also applicable to space environments) and
adjustable mass flow techniques to simulate trajectories. Refined combined cycle propulsion systems (integral
systems that include air turbo rockets, both ramjet / scramjet engines and solid propellant boosters), to advance
the prototypes of HS/HM systems, specifically targeting eventual mission effectiveness.
FY 2026 Plans: Continue improvement to test advanced materials and aerodynamic/control technologies,
including Thermal solutions for advanced materials (also applicable to space environments) and Adjustable
mass flow techniques to simulate trajectories. Continue refinement of the combined cycle propulsion systems
(integral systems that include air turbo rockets, both ramjet / scramjet engines and solid propellant boosters), to
advance the prototypes of HS/HM systems, specifically targeting eventual mission effectiveness. Develop large
scale ground test tunnel at Redstone Arsenal.
Congressional Add: High Powered Microwave Munition                                                                            -       4.500
FY 2026 Plans: Start the Electromagnetic/3D modeling, development of a novel antenna, high power/frequency
component connectors, RF sources and transmitter assemblies. Plan for future system integration.
                                                                                  Congressional Adds Subtotals            7.000       9.500

N/A
Remarks
SMDCoE strategic missile defense capability development efforts have a natural association and linkage with Army Space and High Altitude (SHA) capability
development also performed within the SMDCoE. Emerging space and high altitude technologies and concepts often influence SMD identification, tracking and
response.

Integration

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This Program Element (PE) funds the United States Army Space and Missile Defense Command (USASMDC) development activities, and employment of global space
and high-altitude (SHA) capabilities to the Army, joint force, allies and partners, to enable multi-domain combat effects; enhance deterrence, assurance, and detection
of strategic attacks; and protect the Nation. The USASMDC is the warfighting function lead and Department of the Army force modernization proponent for integration
of current and future SHA systems to enable Army forces on the battlefield. The USASMDC workforce supports the research and doctrine development from one of the
USASMDC principal locations in Huntsville, AL; Colorado Springs, CO; and Joint Base Langley-Eustis. Employing cutting-edge technology and incorporating feedback
from the warfighter, the command develops critical space and high-altitude capabilities to maintain overmatch of the nation's near-peer adversaries and to deter, deny
and defeat any challenge. Headquarters, Department of the Army General Order 37, dated 16 October 2006, designated USASMDC as the Army proponent for space,
the Army integrator for global missile defense (GMD), and the Army Service Component Command (ASCC) of the USSTRATCOM. Army Regulation (AR) 10-87, Army
Commands, Army Service Component Commands, and Direct Reporting Units, dated 4 September 2007, and AR 5-22, The Army Force Modernization Proponent
System, dated 19 August 2009, designated USASMDCas the Army specified proponent for Space/High Altitude capabilities. As the Army proponent for space and
high altitude, USASMDC is responsible for developing warfighting concepts, conduct warfighting experiments to validate those concepts, identify capabilities needed
to implement the validated concepts, and develop Doctrine, Organizations, Training, Material, Leadership & Education, Personnel, Facilities and Policy (DOTMLPF-P)
solutions.

Note
The SMDC SPECTRE, and Joint Simulation Environment efforts are new additions to the Space And Missile Defense Integration project beginning in FY2026.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This project funds the Space and High Altitude (SHA) Force Development activities of the United States Army Space and Missile Defense Command (USASMDC)
Space and Missile Defense Center of Excellence (SMDCoE) and Technical Center (TC). The SMDCoE is the warfighting function lead and Department of the Army
force modernization proponent to provide concept development and capabilities determination and integration relative to Doctrine, Organizations, Training, Material,
Leadership & Education, Personnel, Facilities and Policy (DOTMLPF-P) enabling the Army to leverage the SHA domains in support of warfighting operations. As
the Army proponent for SHA, the SMDCoE is responsible for developing capabilities for Army space and high-altitude mission areas, designing and documenting
future space organizations, designing performing and executing wargames and experiments, and providing modeling and simulations for Army space and cyber
technologies from concept to fielding. Effective integration of SHA capabilities enable the application of strategic land power and execution of Multi-Domain Operations
(MDO). Additionally, SHA capabilities anchor the Army's ability to penetrate and disintegrate enemy anti-access and area denial (A2AD) systems and exploit the
resultant freedom of maneuver to achieve strategic objectives and force a return to competition on favorable terms. The SMDCoE workforce supports the research
and doctrine development from one of the SMDCoE principal locations in Huntsville, AL; Colorado Springs, CO; and Joint Base Langley-Eustis. Under the direction
of an experienced member of the Senior Executive Service (SES), the SMDCoE receives guidance from the USASMDC Commanding General and works in close
coordination with the Army's Transformation and Training Command, the United States Strategic Command, the United States Space Command and the Missile
Defense Agency.

Title: Joint Friendly Force Tracking (J-FFT) Testbed                                                                                       3.426       1.701         0.659
Description: Development and deployment of J-FFT capabilities.

FY 2026 Plans:
JFFT Testbed will continue to develop critical materiel solutions and enhancements to ensure ever improving situational
awareness, and improved command and control capabilities for the warfighter. This development supports combat operations
and special tracking needs of CCMDs and other USG users in every theater of operations in order to maximize lethality and
minimize fratricide. J-FFT also serves as JS/J6 technical lead for efforts to create a NATO Common Operational Picture (COP)
necessary to conduct multi-national combat operations.
FY 2027 Plans:
JFFT Testbed will continue to develop critical materiel solutions and enhancements to ensure ever improving situational
awareness, and improved command and control capabilities for the warfighter. This development supports combat operations and
special tracking needs of CCMDs and other USG users in every theater of operations in order to maximize lethality and minimize
fratricide. J-FFT also serves as JS/J6 technical lead for efforts to create a NATO Common Operational Picture (COP) necessary
to conduct multi-national combat operations.

Title: Assured Positioning, Navigation and Timing / Navigation Warfare (A-PNT/NAVWAR)                                                         2.302       1.380         0.898
Description: Provide PNT/NAVWAR capability development support for the Army.

FY 2026 Plans:
Contribute to Army's focus on synchronizing Kinetic and non-kinetic fires and space-based capablities through efforts to mitigate
PNT capability gaps, to provide situational awareness of the NAVWAR environment, and to prevent adversary use of PNT
information through coordinated employment of NAVWAR capabilities. Army Space and Missile Defense Command will continue
to fulfill capability development responsibilities to the All-Domain Sensing (ADS) Cross Functional Team (CFT) and Army Service
Component Command duties in support of USSPACECOM to provide PNT/NAVWAR capabilities to maintain PNT overmatch.
FY 2027 Plans:
Contribute to Army's focus on synchronizing Kinetic and non-kinetic fires and space-based capabilities through efforts to mitigate
PNT capability gaps, to provide situational awareness of the NAVWAR environment, and to prevent adversary use of PNT
information through coordinated employment of NAVWAR capabilities. Army Space and Missile Defense Command will continue
to fulfill capability development responsibilities to the All-Domain Sensing (ADS) Cross Functional Team (CFT) and Army Service
Component Command duties in support of USSPACECOM to provide PNT/NAVWAR capabilities to maintain PNT overmatch.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
The reduction in funding for the A-PNT/NAVWAR effort in FY 2027 reflects two key factors: an overall decrease in Project 990
funding and the SMDCoE's resource realignment. This realignment is part of the Command's restructuring to better support the
Army Transformation Initiative and enhance the delivery of critical warfighting capabilities to the warfighter.

Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                              Date: April 2026

2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 4: Advanced           PE 0603327A / Air and Missile Defense Systems Engineering
Component Development & Prototypes (ACD&P)
                                    Prior                             FY 2027    FY 2027      FY 2027                                            Cost To          Total
      COST ($ in Millions)
                                    Years       FY 2025    FY 2026     Base       OOC          Total    FY 2028    FY 2029    FY 2030   FY 2031 Complete          Cost
Total Program Element                       -     30.000    100.000         -           -           -         -           -         -           -   Continuing Continuing
FG9: Air and Missile Defense                -     30.000    100.000         -           -           -         -           -         -           -   Continuing Continuing
(AMD) Electronic Warfare

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Funding in this program supports Cyber and Electromagnetic Activities (CEMA) and Deep CEMA efforts to conduct realistic assessments of Army Integrated Fires
performance, identify system vulnerabilities, and develop mitigations against threats across the Cyber and Electromagnetic spectrum. Army radars and sensors,
integrated air and missile defense mission command and fire control, Radio Frequency (RF) data and voice networks, and Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT)
technology will be assessed against current and postulated threat systems and techniques. Potential solutions developed by the Army, other Services, and Defense
agencies (for example Missile Defense Agency) to close identified gaps will be demonstrated and assessed in live and simulated CEMA environments. Assessment
events will be conducted approximately every two years. Implementation of potential solutions will occur between events using system-specific funding. The proposed
solutions will then be assessed at the next event after implementation.

 Included in this line are funds to plan and execute periodic CEMA and Deep CEMA activities, in conjunction with Air and Missile Defense and Long-Range Cross
 Functional Teams to support the Army Integrated Fires system, to include other Service and other Agency radar and sensor systems as appropriate. Funding will be
 used to develop solutions to protect Army weapon systems from emerging and future CEMA threats such as advanced Electronic Warfare techniques, Radio Frequency-
 enabled cyber effects, use of photonics, etc. Efforts in this program will also develop tools for use by Army radar and sensor systems to improve overall system
 performance in contested environments, to include effects-based CEMA Modeling and Simulation (M&S) to assess Army CEMA concepts in Hardware-In-The-Loop
 (HWIL) environment. Additionally, virtual models of critical hardware and software are being developed and implemented to allow for destructive testing with advanced
 CEMA threats in a lab environment. There will be continual interface with intelligence communities to maintain cognizance of emerging CEMA threats and incorporate
 these threats in future CEMA demonstrations. These activities follow a time-phased roadmap that identifies the investments needed to improve the resiliency of Army
 radar and sensors, C2, and RF data and voice networks in contested CEMA environments.

 Deep CEMA efforts support assessment of quantum-based hardware, development of software algorithms, and will integrate cutting-edge technology prototypes into
 Army weapon systems for advanced experimentation and assessment.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Funding in this program supports Cyber and Electromagnetic Activities (CEMA) and Deep CEMA efforts to conduct realistic assessments of Army Integrated Fires
performance, identify system vulnerabilities, and develop mitigations against threats across the Cyber and Electromagnetic spectrum. Army radars and sensors,
integrated air and missile defense mission command and fire control, Radio Frequency (RF) data and voice networks, and Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT)
technology will be assessed against current and postulated threat systems and techniques. Potential solutions developed by the Army, other Services, and Defense
agencies (for example Missile Defense Agency) to close identified gaps will be demonstrated and assessed in live and simulated CEMA environments. Assessment
events will be conducted approximately every two years. Implementation of potential solutions will occur between events using system-specific funding. The proposed
solutions will then be assessed at the next event after implementation.

 Included in this line are funds to plan and execute periodic CEMA and Deep CEMA activities, in conjunction with Air and Missile Defense and Long-Range Cross
 Functional Teams to support the Army Integrated Fires system, to include other Service and other Agency radar and sensor systems as appropriate. Funding will be
 used to develop solutions to protect Army weapon systems from emerging and future CEMA threats such as advanced Electronic Warfare techniques, Radio Frequency-
 enabled cyber effects, use of photonics, etc. Efforts in this program will also develop tools for use by Army radar and sensor systems to improve overall system
 performance in contested environments, to include effects-based CEMA Modeling and Simulation (M&S) to assess Army CEMA concepts in Hardware-In-The-Loop
 (HWIL) environment. Additionally, virtual models of critical hardware and software are being developed and implemented to allow for destructive testing with advanced
 CEMA threats in a lab environment. There will be continual interface with intelligence communities to maintain cognizance of emerging CEMA threats and incorporate
 these threats in future CEMA demonstrations. These activities follow a time-phased roadmap that identifies the investments needed to improve the resiliency of Army
 radar and sensors, C2, and RF data and voice networks in contested CEMA environments.

 Deep CEMA efforts support assessment of quantum-based hardware, development of software algorithms, and will integrate cutting-edge technology prototypes into
 Army weapon systems for advanced experimentation and assessment.

 The Department of War (DOW) requires a method to deliver new Fires Technology prototypes to Soldiers more rapidly, as the current process takes approximately 10
 years. The Fires Technology Accelerator Prototype (FTAP) project aims to significantly shorten this development cycle by creating an adaptable, common platform that
 can be quickly updated based on soldier feedback and testing at events like the Concept Focused Warfighting Event (CFWE) and Project Convergence. The primary
 goal is to develop a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) that can be iteratively and rapidly upgraded, ensuring the warfighter receives cutting-edge capabilities efficiently.


 This line does not have a funding request for FY 2027.

Development

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Projects CE5 - The XM123 Autonomous Breaching System (ABS), previously referred to as Ground Obstacle Breaching Lane Neutralizer (GOBLN), will enhance
Combat Engineer capabilities by providing a system to defeat current and emerging threats - including mines and other explosive hazards - improving force survivability
and mobility. Collaborating with Transformation In Contact (TIC) units, the program will mature advanced breaching technologies in Precision Detection, Precision
Neutralization, Area Reduction, and Mission Planning, leveraging advancements in detection and Automated Target Recognition (ATR) for both surface laid and surface
flush hazards as well as maturation of detection capabilities for buried hazards. ABS's modular design will enable integration with air and ground autonomy platforms,
reducing Soldier exposure to risk. The FY 2027 request supports accelerated development and integration of precision detection and neutralization components and
software with candidate autonomous platforms to evaluate Area Reduction capabilities and further develops mature component technologies.


 Project EK7 Area Denial Capability Development provides for the advanced capability development of Terrain Shaping systems and develops modernized, non-
 persistent U.S. Anti-personnel landmine policy compliant munition fields. During joint high intensity conflicts, Terrain Shaping systems disrupt, fix, turn and block enemy
 freedom of maneuver while enhancing friendly freedom of maneuver within the same battle space. Terrain Shaping systems enable maneuver commanders to directly
 influence where battlefield engagements occur. Terrain Shaping systems will replace a portion of the Family of Scatterable Mines (FASCAM) systems which are beyond
 their designed life. The project will develop Terrain Shaping prototype systems leveraging an accelerated agile acquisition strategy that includes rapid prototyping
 followed by system of system demonstration, collaboration and fielding to Transformation in Contact (TiC) units to mature Terrain Shaping technologies in real time
 to drive innovation, enhance learning, refine requirements, and accelerate solution development. The Terrain Shaping systems will use an open system and modular
 approach to leverage existing Army infrastructure and capabilities to include Command, Control, Communication, Computers, Cyber, Intelligence, Surveillance and
 Reconnaissance (C5ISR) platforms, Robotic Ground Vehicles, and Unmanned Aerial Systems. The FY2027 request supports the rapid prototyping effort and delivery of
 initial capability to TiC units to inform a follow-on prototyping effort and production decision.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The XM123 Autonomous Breaching System (ABS), previously referred to as Ground Obstacle Breaching Lane Neutralizer (GOBLN), will enhance Combat Engineer
capabilities by providing a system to defeat current and emerging threats - including mines and other explosive hazards - improving force survivability and mobility.
Collaborating with Transformation In Contact (TiC) units, the program will mature advanced breaching technologies in Precision Detection, Precision Neutralization, Area
Reduction, and Mission Planning, leveraging advancements in detection and Automated Target Recognition (ATR) for both surface laid and surface flush hazards as
well as maturation of detection and neutralization capabilities for buried hazards. ABS's modular design will enable integration with air and ground autonomy platforms,
reducing Soldier exposure to risk. The FY 2027 request supports accelerated development and integration of precision detection and neutralization components and
software with candidate autonomous platforms to evaluate Area Reduction capabilities and further develops mature component technologies.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                    FY 2025            FY 2026       FY 2027
Title: XM123 Autonomous Breaching System (ABS)                                                                                            16.544             12.633        18.085
Description: Develop the Next Generation Mounted Breaching capability to engage near-peer current and emerging threat
obstacles.

FY 2026 Plans:
FY 2026 will continue the industry prototyping effort to conclude with prototype demonstrations and deliver automated threat
detection capabilities to Transformation in Contact (TiC) units by the end of the Fiscal Year.
FY 2027 Plans:
FY 2027 will continue to provide accelerated prototype hardware to Transformation in Contact (TIC) units for feedback to refine
the system design. The program will prepare documentation for Milestone B approval, award Engineering and Manufacturing
Development (EMD) contract(s) to refine system design, build prototype hardware, and integrate detection/neutralization
hardware. The program will also work to evaluate transitioning from a Major Capability Acquisition Pathway to Rapid Prototyping
and Fielding.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
Increase required to support delivery of hardware and software for detection and neutralization development and autonomous
platform integration activities.

C. Other Program Funding Summary ($ in Millions)
N/A
Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
The XM123 ABS program plans to award Engineering and Manufacturing Development (EMD) contract(s) in FY 2027 to develop and prototype a system post Milestone
B. The program is also looking to provide accelerated capabilities and prototype hardware to Transformation In Contact (TiC) units to assess and rapidly innovate
automated software and hardware to refine system design, and integrate detection and neutralization hardware and software with autonomy platforms. Feedback from
TiC units for this hardware and software will inform follow-on system design and production decision to provide capabilities to additional TiC units. Prototype systems
in EMD will leverage mature detection and neutralization capabilities, available common architectures, platform autonomy, Automated Target Recognition, and TiC
unit feedback. A competitive assessment of government-developed and industry solutions will inform the selection of best capabilities for integration into a complete
breaching system. Capabilities will be provided to TiC units as Minimum Viable Product (MVP) available to support testing and qualify safety critical systems to support
production decision and fielding to additional units in greater quantities. Following EMD and a Milestone C in FY2030, Low Rate Initial Production (LRIP) will begin in
FY2031, supporting operational testing the following year, with Initial Operational Capability (IOC) projected for FY2032 and Full Material Release planned for FY2033.
The program will also assess rapid prototyping and fielding results to inform potential schedule adjustments.

Exhibit R-2A, RDT&E Project Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                                      Date: April 2026

2040 / 4                                                                         PE 0603619A / Landmine Warfare and Barri EK7 / Area Denial Capability Development

                                    Prior                             FY 2027     FY 2027       FY 2027                                               Cost To           Total
     COST ($ in Millions)
                                    Years       FY 2025    FY 2026     Base        OOC           Total     FY 2028     FY 2029     FY 2030   FY 2031 Complete           Cost
EK7: Area Denial Capability                 -     39.933     21.663     13.289           -        13.289     28.551       11.221         -           -     Continuing Continuing
Development
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -          -            -            -          -            -          -           -

Note
Project EK7 funding supports the Terrain Shaping Capability Abbreviated-Capability Development Document Update (A-CDD-U) with an updated requirement for an
autonomous agnostic Terrain Shaping capability and Beyond Line of Sight control.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project EK7 Area Denial Capability Development provides for the advanced capability development of Terrain Shaping systems and develops modernized, nonpersistent
U.S. Anti-personnel landmine policy compliant munition fields. During joint high intensity conflicts, Terrain Shaping systems disrupt, fix, turn and block enemy
freedom of maneuver while enhancing friendly freedom of maneuver within the same battle space. Terrain Shaping systems enable maneuver commanders to directly
influence where battlefield engagements occur. Terrain Shaping systems will replace a portion of the Family of Scatterable Mines (FASCAM) systems which are beyond
their designed life. The project will develop Terrain Shaping prototype systems leveraging an accelerated agile acquisition strategy that includes rapid prototyping
followed by system of system demonstration, collaboration and fielding to Engineer Brigade units to mature Terrain Shaping technologies in real time to drive innovation,
enhance learning, refine requirements, and accelerate solution development. The Terrain Shaping systems will use an open system and modular approach to leverage
existing Army infrastructure and capabilities to include Command, Control, Communication, Computers, Cyber, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C5ISR)
platforms, Robotic Ground Vehicles, and Unmanned Aerial Systems. The FY2027 request supports the rapid prototyping effort and delivery of initial capability to
Engineer Brigade units to inform a follow-on prototyping effort and production decision.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                     FY 2025          FY 2026     FY 2027
Title: Terrain Shaping Capability                                                                                                          38.590           21.663      13.289
Description: Develop, integrate, mature and demonstrate Terrain Shaping solutions to support the approved Terrain Shaping
Capability Abbreviated-Capability Development Document Update (A-CDD-U). The Terrain Shaping program aims to develop
an effective complex obstacle capable of blocking, turning, fixing, and disrupting enemy maneuver. The desired characteristics
for this capability include disabling a main battle tank, compliance with the national landmine policy, a 12-month prototype
demonstration, autonomous delivery and emplacement, and a live ordnance free training capability. Furthermore, a robust
command and control system shall be provided, capable of transmitting, receiving, and controlling the obstacles. A planning tool
will also be provided to plan and track obstacle employment location and the lifespan of effectors within a common operating
picture. These desired characteristics will guide the prototyping effort, enhancing the overall obstacle strategy and operational
effectiveness.

FY 2026 Plans:

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                   FY 2025      FY 2026       FY 2027
Award up to 4 prototyping contracts that will seek to develop, integrate, and mature a prototype system that achieves Technology
Readiness Level (TRL) 7 at a demonstration event 12 months after the OTA award. Conclude with decision point for approval to
authorize Engineer Brigade validation phase or continue prototyping and technical maturation.
FY 2027 Plans:
Deliver prototypes to Engineer Brigade units to validate the systems' operational effectiveness. Conduct formal Government
qualification and safety testing to ensure the Terrain Shaping systems are safe, suitable, and effective for operational use.
Conclude with a soldier-operated live-fire validation exercise, confirming the system's operational readiness. Conclude with
decision point for approval to continue to rapid fielding or continue prototyping.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
FY 2026 funding for the XM250 Close Terrain Shaping Obstacle was redirected after the Army Acquisition Executive terminated
the program. Following a Decision Authority Acquisition Decision Memorandum (ADM) codifying the Terrain Shaping Capability
2QFY26 Acquisition Shaping Panel, all $21M of the FY 2026 funds will support the award of up to 4 prototyping contracts. FY
2027 funding levels will support Terrain Shaping prototype contract maturation, test and validation efforts.
Title: XM250 Terrain Shaping Obstacles Capability Development                                                                              1.343             -            -
Description: Orderly shutdown of the XM250 program and termination of the Other Transaction Authority (OTA)

D. Acquisition Strategy
The Terrain Shaping Capability Abbreviated-Capability Development Document Army Requirements Oversight Council (AROC) and subsequent Decision Authority
Acquisition Decision Memorandum (ADM) approval is expected in 2QFY2026. The Terrain Shaping development strategy will leverage an accelerated agile acquisition
strategy. Initial rapid prototyping will allow us to define the materiel solution and concept of employment to inform the CDD in FY2027. The award of multiple rapid
prototyping contracts will seek a system solution demonstration in 12 months. The strategy will seek to leverage other Army S&T ongoing efforts as well as existing Army

SYSTEM

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Screening Obscuration Module (SOM) is a rapidly deployable modular system that can disrupt electromagnetic sensors used for identification and targeting. SOM can
be deployed on multiple platforms ranging from fielded platforms to future unmanned robotic platforms. The SOM strategy includes an incremental approach: SOM-
Visual (SOM-V, increment one), SOM-Bispectral (SOM-B, increment two), and SOM-Multispectral (SOM-M, increment three). SOM-M covers the full spectrum of visual
through millimeter wave screening obscuration; covering all three incremental capabilities. SOM benefits the warfighter by disrupting adversaries targeting systems and
improving soldier and platform survivability.

 The SOM-M program will utilize FY 2027 RDTE to award a contract and initiate a preliminary design in order to develop prototypes and initiate risk reduction testing.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Screening Obscuration Module (SOM) is a rapidly deployable modular system that can disrupt electromagnetic sensors used for identification and targeting. SOM can
be deployed on multiple platforms ranging from fielded platforms to future unmanned robotic platforms. The SOM strategy includes an incremental approach: SOM-
Visual (SOM-V, increment one), SOM-Bispectral (SOM-B, increment two), and SOM-Multispectral (SOM-M, increment three). SOM-M covers the full spectrum of visual
through millimeter wave screening obscuration; covering all three incremental capabilities. SOM benefits the warfighter by disrupting adversaries targeting systems and

 The SOM-M program will utilize FY 2027 RDTE to award a contract and initiate a preliminary design in order to develop prototypes and initiate risk reduction testing.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                   FY 2025          FY 2026     FY 2027
Title: Screening Obscuration Module (SOM) Multispectral (M) Development                                                                      -                -        5.596
Description: Maturation, prototyping, and risk reduction testing of SOM Multispectral (SOM-M) (Increment three). Delivers
Visual, Infrared and Millimeter Wave (MMW) obscuration screening capability to counter Unmanned Ariel System (UAS) targeting
systems. Provides obscuration and screening capability in a modular system which can be used on a range of manned and
unmanned platforms. This includes hardening the dissemination system and scaling up the manufacturing process for the
screening materiel.

FY 2027 Plans:
Initiate maturation and implementation of multispectral screening technology into one dissemination box and down selection of
screening materiel's. Risk reduction testing and conducing a preliminary design review will support Milestone B decision and entry
into Engineering and Manufacturing Development (EMD) phase.

FY 2027 funding increase supports initiation of SOM Multispectral (SOM-M) development, increment three of the existing SOM
CDD.

C. Other Program Funding Summary ($ in Millions)
Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
The SOM Multispectral (SOM-M) effort is comprised of development activities that include prototyping, field testing, and user touch points which supports fielding critical
capabilities, while Science & Technology (S&T) efforts continue the maturation of emerging technologies. The SOM strategy includes an incremental approach: SOM-
Visual (SOM-V, increment one), SOM-Bispectral (SOM-B, increment two), and SOM-Multispectral (SOM-M, increment three). SOM-M will acquire capability through a
tailored acquisition approach that allows prototyping and procurement of available capabilities while continuing to insert emerging S&T efforts. The SOM-M contracting
strategy is accomplished through flexible contracting awards via Other Transaction Authority (OTA) contract/agreement, to assess and develop vendor technologies for
prototype and system architecture.

Note
The FY 2026 spend plan amount for Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition includes $87,972 thousand of discretionary and $50,000 thousand of mandatory
(reconciliation) for a total of $137,972 thousand.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
A portion of this funding line is directly aligned to each Future Vertical Lift (FVL) and Assured Positioning, Navigation, & Timing (APNT) Army Modernization Priorities.
The Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition Program Element encompasses a comprehensive program to develop, rapidly transition to production, and field advanced
weapons and munitions for small, medium and large caliber munitions, tank ammunition, mortar ammunition, cannon artillery ammunition, and close combat system
items. These Projects will ensure continued battlefield overmatch and lethality of United States maneuver forces against the full range of modern battlefield threats.


PE 0603639A: Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition                             UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                   Volume 2a - 56
Army                                                                              Page 1 of 64                         R-1 Line #63

===== PAGE 89 =====
                                                                             UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                                   Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                    R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)
2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 4: Advanced            PE 0603639A / Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition
Component Development & Prototypes (ACD&P)
 To achieve this, Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition projects will identify and develop promising technologies through competitive development and streamlined
 acquisition procedures.
 .
 Project CD8 - Long Range Precision Munition (LRPM) is the lethal variant of the Launched Effects (LE) Family of Systems which provide a range of capabilities through
 variations of platforms, payloads, and mission systems. LE provides tactical and operational flexibility through collaboration of heterogeneous groups of LE launched
 from multiple domains (land and air) in conjunction with other manned platforms, C2, unmanned systems, and sensors throughout the operational environment. The
 ability to interoperate and coordinate with other LE systems at long ranges and adapt to changing threats is a core concept of the Launched Effects Abbreviated
 Capability Development Document validated in June 2024. Primary target set for LRPM is Integrated Air Defense Systems. LRPM will provide Army Aviation and Ground
 Forces with a precise long range munition system to rapidly respond in a combat environment to improve the lethality and stand-off of Warfighters and aviation platforms
 in an Anti-Access Area Denial (A2AD) and positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) denied environment.

Project DK5 - The 155 millimeter (mm) High Explosive Conventional Artillery Modernization will develop common artillery projectiles capable of accurate delivery of
multiple payloads to provide US Army's Field Artillery units with lethal and nonlethal area effects at ranges necessary to disable and destroy a broad set of targets
within multiple threat formations. The Modular Artillery for Combat Effectiveness (MACE) line of effort will develop lethal area effects. The Extended Range (ER)
Cargo line of effort will develop lethal and non-lethal areas effects at the longer ranges necessary to enable transformation in contact. This effort is executing an
evolutionary approach to ensure a common projectile can support multiple cargo capabilities, including but not limited to lethal, Radio Frequency (RF) obscuration,
visible obscuration, Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR), Infra-red (IR) illumination, visible illumination, electronic attack, Terrain Shaping Obstacles,
and future cargo capabilities. Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding will support the Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 6 demonstration, down-selection, contract award for
system development, and technical assessment in support of the Army's Field Artillery modernization objectives.

Project DK7 - The 155mm Artillery Propulsion Modernization (Advanced Component Development) project supports the United States Army's Field Artillery
modernization objectives by developing and maturing three propulsion system components: (1) modular charge that achieves minimum to intermediate ranges, (2)
supercharge that achieves maximum range, and (3) percussion primer that initiates both the modular and supercharges. Development efforts also include the design
of packaging solutions required for protection during transportation and long-term storage, digital engineering, test measurement devices and modeling and simulation
tools. Design emphasis will shift from Joint Ballistic Memorandum of Understanding (JBMOU) compatibility to down selected Mobile Tactical Cannon (MTC) that
maximizes interoperability with foreign allies, ensuring that the components support manufacturability within the National Technology and Industrial Base (NTIB).
Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding will support continued prototyping and testing of propelling charges, combustible cases, additives, and ignition components to advance
technologies that improve propellant effectiveness, extend cannon life, enhance primer and charge ignition performance, optimize system-level handling, rates of fire,
and overall propulsion suitability and survivability.

Project DL5 - The Extended Range Artillery Munitions (ERAM) project supports the development of a 155mm system of systems that includes projectile, propellant,
primer, fuze, and fuze setter which will deliver lethality and range overmatch in 155mm artillery weapon systems at more than double the current range from legacy
artillery cannons and will be compatible with future 155mm artillery systems in contested Global Positioning System (GPS) environments and suboptimal atmospheric
conditions. The ERAM development effort is part of an organic Long Range Precision Fires capability, which will provide overmatching cannon artillery range capability
at both Tactical and operational Fires range by shaping the nature of the close fight through seeking moving and imprecisely located targets at extended ranges,


PE 0603639A: Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition                              UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                Volume 2a - 57
Army                                                                            Page 2 of 64                         R-1 Line #63

                                                                            UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                                Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                      R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)
2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 4: Advanced              PE 0603639A / Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition
Component Development & Prototypes (ACD&P)
 increase range capability of the current 39 caliber cannon fleet and will also be compatible with future 52 caliber and above artillery weapon systems. Fiscal Year (FY)
 2027 funding will support technology maturation and risk reduction of key system and subsystems improvements in performance in difficult use cases (such as GPS
 contested environments and suboptimal atmospheric conditions), and integration of the tactical warhead and seeker culminating in a series of Design Verification
 Testing (DVT) to achieve Technology Readiness Level (TRL) six (6) maturity. The FY 2026 President's Budget request included $50.000 million of mandatory funding.

Project DN7 - The Mobile-Long Range Precision Strike Missile (M-LRPSM) is an approved Directed Requirement (DR). It must defeat Tier 1 thru Tier 3 Armor (stationary
& defilade) Troops, Field Fortifications & Urban structures, and achieve a range of greater than 25 KM. It will be transportable by existing Infantry Brigade Combat
Teams (IBCT) Light Tactical Vehicle platforms. It has the ability to adjust the missile flight, retarget, and abort, and have multiple missile launch capability. It must be
survivable and resilient in denied and degraded environments.

Project EC3 - Ammunition Logistics Prototyping - This project supports the future force by improving the distribution, management, reliability, and survivability of
ammunition through the advanced development, integration, and demonstration of logistics system enablers in alignment with Transformation In Contact and Deliberate
Transformation. These enablers will improve the resiliency, efficiency and effectiveness of ammunition operations, to include retrograde, while reducing the logistics
footprint on the battlefield. Technology areas addressed include handling, distribution, management (strategic and tactical), prognostics, diagnostics, asset visibility,
explosives safety, autonomy friendly packaging, and palletization. The efficient deployment and sustainment of reliable ammunition is vital to success on the battlefield.
This project enhances the operational effectiveness of the ammunition logistics system to ensure the distribution of reliable ammunition to the Warfighter.

Project FA5 - The Assured Precision Weapons and Munitions (APWM) - FA5 Project is focused on advanced risk mitigation, technology integration, expedited
prototyping, and product support to rapidly identify, evaluate, mature, test, and demonstrate various assured precision related prototype technologies in munitions
and munition resilient support and survivability components and subsystems within a complex environment with the overarching goal of solving tough operational
problems and addressing critical capability gaps. The APWM project reinforces the Secretary of War's Acquisition Transformation Strategy, and the Army Transformation
Initiative (ATI) major lines of effort through fast technology development and prototyping, which increases lethality and ensures future combat overmatch success of
the Joint force against peer/near-peer adversaries and the China pacing threat. This project also aims to improve performance and affordability of Army and Joint
capability portfolios via Joint Lethality (JL) Positioning, Navigation and Timing (PNT), Navigation Warfare (NavWar), and Army M-code Global Positioning System (GPS)
coordinated efforts and integration of innovative industry contributions. The APWM project directly supports the top Army and Joint force modernization priorities in
support of the NDS and multiple Public Law (PL) related congressional imperatives. Funding will support engagement by munition PNT and NavWar related Lethality
experts in the development, evaluation, and technology delivery activities of the M-code GPS as well as the Army's continuous adaptation of PNT, NavWar, and
Electronic Warfare (EW) enabled capabilities to achieve accurate extended range and hypersonic lethality, increase warfighter survivability, maximizing precision
munition stowed kills and countering Anti-Access/Area Denial (A2/AD) threats. The APWM project enables component and subsystem architecture input essential
for precision munitions and other such lethality delivery mechanisms operating in a complex EW threat environments, M-code GPS and technology integration and
evaluation (including verifying and validating this capability for future Joint precision munitions), and maturation of alternative PNT and EW/NavWar related technologies,
components, payloads, and solutions. Overall, these efforts contribute to informed Army modernization, portfolio, and milestone decisions enhancing the Department's
capacity to deploy new technologies and capabilities swiftly. The FY 2026 President's Budget request included $20.000 million of mandatory funding.




PE 0603639A: Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition                             UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                             Volume 2a - 58
Army                                                                           Page 3 of 64                        R-1 Line #63

===== PAGE 91 =====
                                                                            UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                                 Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                     R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)
2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 4: Advanced             PE 0603639A / Tank and Medium Caliber Ammunition
Component Development & Prototypes (ACD&P)
 Project FG1 - The Cannon-Delivered Area Effects Munitions (C-DAEM) Budget Activity Four (BA4) project supports the development efforts of the Extended Range
 Artillery Projectile (ERAP), which transitioned in Fiscal Year (FY) 2025 from Budget Activity Three (BA3) PE 0603464A / Long Range Precision Fires Advanced
 Technology Long Range Precision Fires Advanced Tech, Project BO8), will deliver lethality and range overmatch in 155mm artillery weapon systems at more than
 double the current range from legacy artillery cannons and will be compatible in future 155MM artillery systems in a Global Positioning System (GPS) degraded and
 denied environments. ERAP, developed as part of an organic Long Range Precision Fires capability, will provide overmatching cannon artillery range capability at both
 Tactical and Operational Fires range by shaping the nature of the close fight through seeking moving and imprecisely located targets at extended ranges, will increase
 range capability of the current 39 caliber cannon fleet and will also be compatible with future 52 caliber and above artillery weapon systems. This project does not have a
 Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 budget request.

Project XT5 - 30mm Anti-Personnel and Counter Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS): Airburst capability is identified as a threshold Key System Attribute (KSA) in Apache
Block 3 Capability Production Document (CPD) - Approved 14 June 2017 and other cannon caliber Operational Needs Statements (ONS) and Capability Development
Documents (CDD). The Anti-Personnel and Counter Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) munition provides increased lethality through airburst effects against personnel,
small boats, and small Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) without requiring modification to the platform.

CD8: Long Range Precision                   -     30.583      2.500          -            -           -         -           -           -             -         0.000      33.083
Munition (LRPM)
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -          -            -            -         -           -           -             -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Long Range Precision Munition (LRPM) is the lethal variant of the Launched Effects (LE) Family of Systems which provide a range of capabilities through variations
of platforms, payloads, and mission systems. LE provides tactical and operational flexibility through collaboration of heterogeneous groups of LE launched from
multiple domains (land and air) in conjunction with other manned platforms, C2, unmanned systems, and sensors throughout the operational environment. The ability
to interoperate and coordinate with other LE systems at long ranges and adapt to changing threats is a core concept of the Launched Effects Abbreviated Capability
Development Document validated in June 2024. Primary target set for LRPM is Integrated Air Defense Systems. LRPM will provide Army Aviation and Ground Forces
with a precise long range munition system to rapidly respond in a combat environment to improve the lethality and stand-off of Warfighters and aviation platforms in an
Anti-Access Area Denial (A2AD) and positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) denied environment.

Project CD8 - Long Range Precision Munition (LRPM) was transferred to PE 0605345A/Project A56 in FY 2026.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                    FY 2025            FY 2026       FY 2027
Title: Long Range Precision Munition                                                                                                      30.583                 -             -
Description: This line funds the integration and qualification of a LE munition system that will engage and deliver lethal effects
on targets at ranges beyond line of sight. The LRPM Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP) effort includes
demonstration and qualification of a precision guided munition with the capability to complete the assigned mission in cyber-
attack, countermeasures, and anti-access area denial environments. These efforts will include technical assessments, technology
maturation, test and evaluation, demonstration of prototype hardware, system and platform integration, and document preparation
for associated contract and acquisition efforts.

UAS Medium Caliber Ammunition within the Army's Long Range Precision Munitions.

C. Other Program Funding Summary ($ in Millions)
N/A
Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
Previously, LRPM was structured to develop a lethal capability under the Army Aviation Weapons, Sub-Systems, and Munitions Initial Capability Document, validated in
July 2018, to operated under the ecosystem as described in the Future Attack Reconnaissance Aircraft (FARA) Abbreviated Capabilities Development Document (FARA
A-CDD). In FY 2024, the U.S. Army aligned LRPM under the Launched Effects (LE) Family of Systems. The LRPM program was approved by the Army Acquisition
Executive for a Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP) pathway on 9 July 2024.

An award of an U.S. Army Combat Capabilities Development Command Aviation and Missile Center (DEVCOM AvMC) Small Business Innovative Research (SBIR)
Phase III contract in July 2024 enables integration of U.S. Army developed software into the LRPM selected air vehicle, development and integration of LRPM specific
components, procurement of fully configured prototype test assets, test and qualification support, and associated deliverables including engineering drawings and test
reports. Annual funding will be awarded as necessary to complete the qualification and air worthiness test series as required in the MTA RP effort.

Modernization
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                   -          -          -           -            -            -          -            -            -           -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The 155 millimeter (mm) High Explosive Conventional Artillery Modernization will develop common artillery projectiles capable of accurate delivery of multiple payloads
to provide US Army's Field Artillery units with lethal and nonlethal area effects at ranges necessary to disable and destroy a broad set of targets within multiple threat
formations. The Modular Artillery for Combat Effectiveness (MACE) line of effort will develop lethal area effects. The Extended Range (ER) Cargo line of effort will
develop lethal and non-lethal areas effects at the longer ranges necessary to enable transformation in contact. This effort is executing an evolutionary approach
to ensure a common projectile can support multiple cargo capabilities, including but not limited to lethal, Radio Frequency (RF) obscuration, visible obscuration,
Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR), Infra-red (IR) illumination, visible illumination, electronic attack, Terrain Shaping Obstacles, and future cargo
capabilities. Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding will support the Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 6 demonstration, down-selection, contract award for system development,
and technical assessment in support of the Army's Field Artillery modernization objectives.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                          FY 2025          FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: 155mm Modular Artillery for Combat Effectiveness (MACE)                                                                                      -             1.000         22.000
Description: This effort supports the demonstration, development, and validation of 155 millimeter (mm) Conventional Artillery
lethal cargo ammunition. These development efforts will include the design, prototyping, and evaluation of artillery projectiles that
deliver cargo.

FY 2026 Plans:
Vendors to provide deliverable(s) that develop technical feasibility, while supporting transition to preliminary design.
FY 2027 Plans:
FY 2027 funding will support technology maturation and munition development, while procuring test hardware to support lethal
payload integration.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
FY 2027 funding increase is due to increase in test activities required to support initial integration and assessment of potential
lethal payloads into cargo projectiles.
Title: 155mm Extended Range (ER) Cargo                                                                                                                -           0.155          5.213

C. Other Program Funding Summary ($ in Millions)
N/A
Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
155mm Conventional Artillery Modernization will utilize various contract vehicles such as Department of War Ordnance Technology Consortium (DOTC) Other
Transaction Agreement (OTA) initiatives, Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR), existing OTAs and Government Agreements to prototype and evaluate projectile
alternatives while assessing cargo options as system solutions. Additional capable payloads over time will be integrated as technology matures and becomes available
for system qualification.

                                    Prior                             FY 2027      FY 2027       FY 2027                                                Cost To             Total

                                    Years       FY 2025   FY 2026      Base         OOC           Total     FY 2028     FY 2029    FY 2030     FY 2031 Complete             Cost
DK7: 155mm Artillery Propulsion             -         -      10.341     24.717            -        24.717     13.080           -           -           -          0.000      48.138
Mod - Adv Component Dev
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -           -            -            -          -            -          -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The 155mm Artillery Propulsion Modernization (Advanced Component Development) project supports the United States Army's Field Artillery modernization objectives
by developing and maturing three propulsion system components: (1) modular charge that achieves minimum to intermediate ranges, (2) supercharge that achieves
maximum range, and (3) percussion primer that initiates both the modular and supercharges. Development efforts also include the design of packaging solutions
required for protection during transportation and long-term storage, digital engineering, test measurement devices and modeling and simulation tools. Design emphasis
will shift from Joint Ballistic Memorandum of Understanding (JBMOU) compatibility to down selected Mobile Tactical Cannon (MTC) that maximizes interoperability with
foreign allies, ensuring that the components support manufacturability within the National Technology and Industrial Base (NTIB). Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding will
support continued prototyping and testing of propelling charges, combustible cases, additives, and ignition components to advance technologies that improve propellant
effectiveness, extend cannon life, enhance primer and charge ignition performance, optimize system-level handling, rates of fire, and overall propulsion suitability and
survivability.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                       FY 2025          FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: 155mm Artillery Propulsion Modernization - Advanced Component Development                                                                 -            10.341         24.717
Description: Development and maturation of components, packaging solutions, digital engineering, test measurement devices
and modeling and simulation tools for three 155mm propulsion systems: (1) modular charge, (2) supercharge and (3) percussion
primer.

FY 2026 Plans:
In FY 2026, funding will support prototyping and testing of propelling charge and ignition components to advance technology
for improvement of the propellant effectiveness, cannon life, primer and charge ignition performance, system-level handling and
rates-of-fire, and overall propulsion suitability and survivability.
FY 2027 Plans:
Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding will support continued prototyping and testing of propelling charges, combustible cases, additives,
and ignition components to advance technologies and characterize performance with the down-selected Mobile Tactical Cannon
(MTC) Platform.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:

                                                                                                                                                                   Volume 2a - 72

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                FY 2025      FY 2026      FY 2027
Increase in funding in FY 2027 due to increase in contract and testing costs associated with planned propulsion component
development activities.

Remarks
RDTE Budget Activity 5 (BA5) Program Element 0604802A, DK8 - Weapons and Munitions Engineering Development 155mm Artillery Propulsion Mod - Sys
Demonstration, has been established to support Engineering Manufacturing and Development efforts. Procurement of Ammunition, Army (PAA) budget line items,
Standard Study Numbers E27153 and E27514 have been established to resource 155mm Extended Range Modular Charge and 155mm Extended Range Primer
production requirements.

D. Acquisition Strategy
This effort leverages a Department of War Ordnance Technology Consortium (DOTC) Other Transaction Agreement (OTA) initiatives and Indefinite Delivery Indefinite
Quantity (IDIQ) contracts to drive technology development and integration. These vehicles will support component-level design and manufacturing to final assembly
allowing verification of components, subsystem, and system level maturation through engineering design and testing processes that will maximize risk mitigation
opportunities.

DL5: 155mm Extended Range                    -          -      44.866     139.583            -       139.583     74.452           -          -           -          0.000     258.901
Artillery Munitions
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                   -          -           -           -            -            -          -            -          -           -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The Extended Range Artillery Munitions (ERAM) project supports the development of a 155mm system of systems that includes projectile, propellant, primer, fuse, and
fuse setter which will deliver lethality and range overmatch in 155mm artillery weapon systems at more than double the current range from legacy artillery cannons and
will be compatible with future 155mm artillery systems in contested Global Positioning System (GPS) environments and suboptimal atmospheric conditions. The ERAM
development effort is part of an organic Long Range Precision Fires capability, which will provide overmatching cannon artillery range capability at both Tactical and
operational Fires range by shaping the nature of the close fight through seeking moving and imprecisely located targets at extended ranges, increase range capability
of the current 39 caliber cannon fleet and will also be compatible with future 52 caliber and above artillery weapon systems. Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding will support
technology maturation and risk reduction of key system and subsystems improvements in performance in difficult use cases (such as GPS contested environments
and suboptimal atmospheric conditions), and integration of the tactical warhead and seeker culminating in a series of Design Verification Testing (DVT) to achieve
Technology Readiness Level (TRL) six (6) maturity.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                          FY 2025         FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: Extended Range Artillery Munitions                                                                                                           -           44.866        139.583
Description: The Extended Range Artillery Munitions (ERAM) project supports the development of a 155mm system of systems
that includes projectile, propellant, primer, fuze, and fuze setter which will deliver lethality and range overmatch in 155mm artillery
weapon systems at more than double the current range from legacy artillery cannons and will be compatible with future 155mm
artillery systems in contested Global Positioning System (GPS) environments and suboptimal atmospheric conditions.

FY 2026 Plans:
Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 funding will support technology maturation and risk reduction of key system and subsystems, improvements
in performance in difficult use cases, and integration of the tactical warhead and seeker supporting a series of Design Verification
Testing (DVT) to achieve Technology Readiness Level (TRL) six (6) maturity.
FY 2027 Plans:
Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding will support technology maturation and risk reduction of key system and subsystems improvements
culminating in a Design Verification Test (DVT) to inform a Technical Review. This technical review will form the basis for a down
selection of vendors that have the highest potential to meet cost, schedule, and technical requirements. The program will continue

Budget Activity 5 (BA5) Program Element 0604802A, Weapons and Munitions Engineering Development, Project DL6, 155mm Extended Range Artillery Projectile, has
been established to support Engineering Manufacturing and Development efforts.

D. Acquisition Strategy
The Extended Range Artillery Munitions (ERAM) program will utilize the Middle Tier Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping Pathway and Other Transaction Authority
(OTA) contracting vehicles to execute design, development, and maturation efforts. The U.S. Government is currently evaluating contractor submissions to identify
opportunities to accelerate the schedule and maintain competition to a Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 6 demonstration event. This contracting vehicle will allow
a continuation decision for each technical candidate, resulting in a down select between the prototypes to ensure completion of the demonstration of the candidate
technical solution.

Quantity of RDT&E Articles                    -         -           -           -            -            -          -            -          -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
DN7: The Mobile-Long Range Precision Strike Missile (M-LRPSM) is an approved Directed Requirement (DR). It must defeat Tier 1 thru Tier 3 Armor (stationary &
defilade) Troops, Field Fortifications & Urban structures, and achieve a range of greater than 25 KM. It will be transportable by existing Infantry Brigade Combat Teams
(IBCT) Light Tactical Vehicle platforms. It has the ability to adjust the missile flight, retarget, and abort, and have multiple missiles launch capability. It must be survivable
and resilient in denied and degraded environments.

The FY 2027 funding will provide system prototypes for candidate vendors and their participation in a flyoff demonstration. Following a down select one vendor at the
end of the flyoff demonstration, FY 2027 funding will procure system prototypes to be used to conduct required testing to receive an Urgent Material Release.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                          FY 2025          FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: M-LRPSM Development and Integration                                                                                                          -             5.956         28.082
Description: FY2026 continues to support prototype manufacturing, integration, and test for M-LRPSM.

FY 2026 Plans:
Product improvements based on continuous soldier involvement in the development and fielding of the M-LRPSM.
FY 2027 Plans:
FY2027 funding provides test assets for Vendor 2 and completes qualification testing of Vendor 1 initiated in FY2026
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
The increase in funding represents the fabrication of Vendor 2 system prototypes required for qualification testing and completes
the testing efforts being conducted for Vendor 1
                                                                                     Accomplishments/Planned Programs Subtotals                       -           5.956        28.082

N/A
Remarks
